item.title: 过去一周的 7 大特色 DEV 帖子 | Top 7 Featured DEV Posts from the Past Week
item.description:
发表于每个星期二，我们都会根据流量、参与度和一些编辑策划来汇总前一周的热门帖子。 典型的一周从周一开始，到周日结束，但不用担心，我们会考虑本周晚些时候发布的帖子。

将您的组件运输到任何地方

有时我们希望 React 中组件的某些部分出现在组件树之外。 在这里， @smpnjn向我们展示了如何在任何地方运输我们的组件！ 我们需要做的就是思考门户。

给初级开发者的建议

适合您背景的 DevOps 路线图

想成为一名 DevOps 工程师，但不确定从哪里开始？ 别再看了， @techworld_with_nana已经为 5 种常见背景（从新手到系统管理员）编写了一个路线图。

摆脱 If-Else 链条以延长寿命

所有编程原则和范例都旨在增强代码的可读性和可维护性。 在本文中， @smuhammed分享了一些关于代码寿命的基本技巧。

我们正在与 CSS-in-JS 分手

我们在 React 中获得了一个令人兴奋的新功能，它将使所有事情变得异步，尤其是数据获取，变得更加容易。 使用@dayvster查看所有新功能！

不要傲慢无知

像@wesen一样，我们同意我们都应该渴望让技术更受欢迎。 做到这一点的最好方法是以谦逊、同情、同理心和对技术及其社区的热爱来处理每一种情况。

这就是我们本周二的每周前 7 名！ 请关注本周的 dev.to 以获取每日内容和讨论……并确保在未来关注这个系列。 你可能只是在里面！

一旦暂停，devteam 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，devteam 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，devteam 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 devteam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 devteam 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 devteam 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Michael Tharrington for The DEV Team

Posted on Oct 18

Every Tuesday we round up the previous week's top posts based on traffic, engagement, and a hint of editorial curation. The typical week starts on Monday and ends on Sunday, but don't worry, we take into account posts that are published later in the week.

Transport your Components Anywhere

Sometimes we want certain parts of a component in React to appear outside the component tree. Here, @smpnjn shows us how to transport our components anywhere! All we need to do is think with portals.

Advice for Junior Devs

@jeroendedauw shares some general and technical advice for junior developers, as well as some solid recommendations for supplemental reading.

DevOps Roadmap to Fit Your Background

Looking to become a DevOps engineer but not sure where to start? Look no further, @techworld_with_nana has written a roadmap tailored to 5 common backgrounds (from newbies to sysadmins).

Get Rid of If-Else Chains for Longevity

All programming principles and paradigms aim to enhance code readability and maintainability. In this article, @smuhammed shares some basic tips for code longevity.

We're Breaking Up with CSS-in-JS

@srmagura, the lead developer at Spot, delves into what originally attracted the team to CSS-in-JS, and why they have decided to shift away from it.

React Just Got Even More Awesome

We're getting an exciting new feature in React that will make all things async, especially data fetching, a lot easier. Check out all the new features with @dayvster!

Don't be Arrogantly Ignorant

Like @wesen, we agree that we should all aspire to make technology more welcoming. The best way to do so is to approach every situation with humility, compassion, empathy, and a love of technology & its communities.

That's it for our weekly Top 7 for this Tuesday! Keep an eye on dev.to this week for daily content and discussions...and be sure to keep an eye on this series in the future. You might just be in it!

Once suspended, devteam will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, devteam will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by devteam will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If devteam is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag devteam:

devteam consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging devteam will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: 放弃 Google Analytics 以在 Amazon Lightsail 上进行似是而非的分析 | Ditch Google Analytics for Plausible Analytics on Amazon Lightsail
item.description:
它是一个小型（比 Google Analytics 小 94%）的开源工具，允许用户自行托管或为云托管版本付费。 如果您想要一个易于使用的工具并且担心隐私，或者您不希望 Google 收集您的分析数据，那么这就是您的工具！

如何开始使用似是而非的分析？

你有两个选择：

  1. 我们将专注于如何在 Amazon Lightsail 上运行开源版本

    什么是亚马逊 Lightsail？

    我们将在不到 5 分钟的时间内在 Lightsail 上运行 Plausible。 让我们开始吧！

    登录到 Amazon Lightsail

    如果您还没有 Lightsail 账户，请前往Amazon Lightsail 并创建一个。

    登录服务后，它将如下所示：

    我们将创建一个实例。 点击“创建实例”

    选择您要部署的区域。 目前，您在全球有 14 个区域可以部署您的实例。 我正在使用弗吉尼亚（US-EAST-1a）。

    单击“仅操作系统”按钮并选择最新版本的 Ubuntu（对我来说是 20.04）

    为了加快部署和配置，我们将使用启动脚本，它会在我们登录实例之前安装和配置一些必需的文件。

    单击“添加启动脚本”并粘贴以下代码：

     sudo apt update -y curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu focal stable" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list > /dev/null sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install docker-ce docker-ce-cli docker-compose containerd.io -y sudo mkdir /var/www cd /var/www sudo git clone https://github.com/plausible/hosting cd hosting sudo git clone https://github.com/TheBrianGraf/AmazonLightsail/ sudo chmod +x ./AmazonLightsail/Plausible-conf-setup.sh

    上面的代码执行以下操作：

    • 更新可用的软件包存储库
    • 下载的 Docker 和其他 Docker 组件
    • 下载的合理设置文件
    • 下载一个合理的配置文件

    选择实例大小

    我们将选择 4GB/2vCPU 实例，但您可以根据需要选择更小或更大的实例。

    最后，我们将为实例命名。 我们称其为“似是而非的分析” 。 点击“创建实例”

    添加静态IP并打开8000端口

    我们希望我们的 Lightsail 实例保持相同的 IP 地址，即使将来重新启动该实例也是如此。 要添加静态 IP，请单击您的实例名称。

    单击“网络”选项卡，然后单击“附加静态 IP”

    单击“创建静态 IP”并为其命名。 我们将其命名为“Plausible-Analytics-IP”。 点击“创建”。

    我们将继续打开 TCP 端口 8000，这样我们就可以在配置完成后访问我们的似是而非的服务器。 再次单击您的实例名称并返回“网络”选项卡。

    在 IPv4 防火墙下，单击“添加规则”。 在端口框中键入“8000”，然后单击“创建”

    现在我们准备好登录到我们的实例并运行最后几个命令来启动和运行。

    登录实例

    向上滚动到页面顶部并单击“连接”选项卡，然后单击“使用 SSH 连接”，这将打开一个新窗口，允许您在实例中运行命令。

    将以下代码复制并粘贴（或输入）到 SSH 终端：

     # Run the config setup file sudo /var/www/hosting/AmazonLightsail/Plausible-conf-setup.sh # Change directories to the Plausible files cd /var/www/hosting # Kick off the Docker container sudo docker-compose up --detach # Wait about 10-20 seconds after the above command completes then run the final command. If it gives an error. Wait another 10 seconds and try it again. # sudo docker-compose exec plausible_db psql -U postgres -d plausible_db -c "UPDATE users SET email_verified = true;"

    运行这些命令后，您将能够登录到 Plausible。 将实例的 IP 地址复制到 Web 浏览器并包含端口 8000。您现在应该能够使用“plausible-conf.env”文件中的凭据登录。

    从这里您可以使用 Plausible 而不是 Google Analytics 开始您的旅程。 有关如何开始添加网站的更多信息，您可以在此处继续设置。



原文:

Plausible is a simple and privacy-friendly alternative to Google Analytics. It's a small (94% smaller than Google Analytics), open-source tool that allows users to either self-host or pay for the cloud-hosted version. If you want an easy-to-use tool and are concerned about privacy or you don't want Google harvesting your analytics data, this is the tool for you!

How can I start using Plausible Analytics?

You have two options:

  1. Plausible.io - Hosted version, paid monthly or yearly, and fully-managed by Plausible.

  1. Plausible.io - Open-source/self-hosted, free, managed by you.

We will be focusing on how to run the open-source version on Amazon Lightsail

What is Amazon Lightsail?

Amazon Lightsail offers easy-to-use virtual private server (VPS) instances, containers, storage, databases, and more at a cost-effective monthly price. Lightsail makes it easy to choose an Operating system or an application blueprint to deploy and use. We will get Plausible running on Lightsail in less than 5 minutes. Let's get started!

Login to Amazon Lightsail

If you do not already have a Lightsail account, head over to Amazon Lightsail and create one.

Once you login to the service, it will look like this:

We are going to create an instance. Click 'Create instance'

Choose the region you want this deployed. Currently you have 14 regions globally where your instances can be deployed. I'm using Virginia (US-EAST-1a).

Click the 'OS Only' button and select the latest version of Ubuntu (for me that is 20.04)

To speed up the deployment and configuration we are going to use a Launch Script, which will install and configure some of the required files before we even log in to the instance.

Click on 'Add Launch Script' and paste the following code:

sudo apt update -y
curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg
echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu focal stable" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list > /dev/null

sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install docker-ce docker-ce-cli docker-compose containerd.io -y

sudo mkdir /var/www
cd /var/www
sudo git clone https://github.com/plausible/hosting
cd hosting
sudo git clone https://github.com/TheBrianGraf/AmazonLightsail/
sudo chmod +x ./AmazonLightsail/Plausible-conf-setup.sh

The above code does the following:

  • Updates available packages repository
  • Download's Docker and additional Docker components
  • Download's Plausible setup files
  • Download's a Plausible config file

Choose an Instance Size

Lightsail offers a number of different sized instances to meet the needs of it's users. We'll choose the 4GB/2vCPU instance but you can choose a smaller or larger instance as you require.

Lastly, we'll give the Instance a name. We'll call ours 'Plausible-Analytics'. Click 'Create instance'

Add a static IP and Open Port 8000

We want our Lightsail Instance to keep the same IP address even if the instance is rebooted in the future. To add a Static IP, Click on the name of your instance.

Click the 'Networking' tab and click 'Attach static IP'

Click 'Create Static IP' and give it a name. We'll name ours 'Plausible-Analytics-IP'. Click 'Create'.

We'll go ahead and open TCP Port 8000 so that we can reach our Plausible server once it's configured. Click on your instance name again and go back to the 'Networking' Tab.

Under IPv4 Firewall, click 'Add rule'. Type '8000' in the Port box and click 'Create'

Now we are ready to login to our instance and run the last few commands to get up and running.

Login to the instance

Scroll back up to the top of the page and click the 'Connect' tab, then click 'Connect using SSH' this will open a new window that allows you to run commands in your instance.

Copy and paste (or type in) the following code into the SSH terminal:

# Run the config setup file
sudo /var/www/hosting/AmazonLightsail/Plausible-conf-setup.sh

# Change directories to the Plausible files
cd /var/www/hosting

# Kick off the Docker container
sudo docker-compose up --detach
# Wait about 10-20 seconds after the above command completes then run the final command. If it gives an error. Wait another 10 seconds and try it again.
# 
sudo docker-compose exec plausible_db psql -U postgres -d plausible_db -c "UPDATE users SET email_verified = true;"

Once you've run these commands, you will be able to login to Plausible. Copy the IP address of your instance into your web browser and include port 8000. You should now be able to login with the credentials found in the 'plausible-conf.env' file.

From here you can begin your journey using Plausible instead of Google Analytics. For more information on how to get started with adding your website, you can continue with your setup HERE.
item.author: thebriangraf

item.title: Sky Cart：使用 Stripe Checkout 的开源、与云无关的购物车 | Sky Cart: An Open Source, cloud-agnostic shopping cart using Stripe Checkout
item.description:
本月早些时候，Josh Nussbaum ( @joshnuss ) 宣布了Sky Cart ，这是一个新的开源项目，它使用 Svelte、Prisma、Vitest 和 Stripe Checkout 将购物车添加到网站：

使用 SvelteKit + Prisma + Vitest + Stripe 构建了一个小型购物车。

用很少的代码将购物车推广到结帐：

通过几行简短的代码，他创建了一个无头、与云无关的购物车嵌入，该嵌入利用了Stripe Checkout提供的托管购买流程。

使用天空车

由于 Sky Cart 是无头的，因此 UI 实现取决于您的 Web 应用程序的框架。 在后台，Sky Cart 使用 Stripe API 提供待售产品列表。 这些可以使用 Sky Cart 的Catalog API在您的站点上提供。

您需要构建 UI 以在您的网站上公开产品列表。 有一些 API 可用于创建购物车，以及添加、删除和更新购物车中的商品。

天空车是如何建造的

如果您以前使用过Svelte ，那么您可能遇到过SvelteKit ，它是用于构建 Svelte 应用程序的最小应用程序框架。 使用 SvelteKit，Sky Cart 的功能是通过无服务器云功能提供的。

由于 SkyCart 的功能是建立在无服务器功能之上的，因此您的 Web 应用程序可以像任何其他无服务器项目一样进行扩展 - 这意味着可以并行运行多个购物车，而不会出现任何问题或性能下降。

测试

使用 Vitest 编写的测试用例位于 repo 的 /test/ 文件夹中 - 查看cart.test.js以查看用于测试的语法示例。

数据层

这又是一个非常简单的实现——您可以在schema.prisma中看到 Sky Cart 的整个对象模型。

条纹结帐

它为用户提供 API 以将商品添加到购物车、删除商品和更新总计。 一旦用户决定进行购买，对Sky Cart 的结账 API 的调用会将用户发送到 Stripe 的托管结账体验，在那里他们可以安全地完成购买。

将 Sky Cart 作为开源软件 (OSS) 提供

由于 SvelteKit 仍然很新，这个项目是深入挖掘 SvelteKit 开发人员体验的好方法。 通过使用 SvelteKit 构建一个简单的工具，Josh 能够为 SvelteKit 团队提供反馈，同时创建一些资源来帮助其他人将 Prisma 与 SvelteKit 集成。

该项目是 Josh 的一个想法的结果，即使用 Stripe 为电子商务网站提供目录和购买功能。 由于 Stripe 管理产品和定价数据以及结帐流程，因此尝试在这两种资源之间添加购物车是有意义的。

在构建 Sky Cart 的过程中，Josh 确定并构建了一些开源实用程序，它们以 NPM 包的形式发布和提供：

如果您使用 SvelteKit 进行构建，请查看以下 OSS 资源：

欢迎投稿

由于 Sky Cart 是完全开源的，因此您可以通过访问 GitHub 的 repo joshnuss/sky-cart来提出新功能、添加新功能或帮助维护它。

试试看！

就是这样！ 您现在可以按照Sky Cart 的 README.md 中的说明将其添加到您的站点。 一定要在推特@joshnuss上关注 Joshua Nussbaum！



原文:

Earlier this month, Josh Nussbaum (@joshnuss) announced Sky Cart, a new open source project, which uses Svelte, Prisma, Vitest and Stripe Checkout to add a shopping cart to a web site:

Built a small shopping cart using SvelteKit + Prisma + Vitest + Stripe.

Took very little code to promote a cart to a checkout:

With a few short lines of code, he created a headless, cloud-agnostic shopping cart embed that takes advantage of the hosted purchase flow provided by Stripe Checkout.

Using Sky Cart

Since Sky Cart is headless, the UI implementation depends on your web app’s framework. Products for Sky Cart are provided through the Stripe API - to get started, create a Stripe account and visit the Products page in the Dashboard to create products for your site.

Under the hood, Sky Cart uses the Stripe API to provide a list of products for sale. These can be made available on your site using Sky Cart’s Catalog API.

You’ll need to build the UI to expose a product list on your site. There are APIs available for creating a cart, as well as adding, removing, and updating items in your cart.

Sky Cart’s README provides additional information about using the library on your own project, as well as instructions for Development and Production deployments.

How Sky Cart was built

SvelteKit

If you’ve used Svelte before, you’ve probably come across SvelteKit, which is a minimal application framework for building Svelte apps. With SvelteKit, the functionality for Sky Cart is provided by way of serverless cloud functions.

Sky Cart uses SvelteKit as an abstraction layer for a variety of common serverless cloud hosting services. SvelteKit allows deployment to providers like CloudFlare, Vercel, and Netlify using plugins called Adapters.

Since functionality for SkyCart is built on top of serverless functions, your web app can scale just like any other serverless project - this means multiple carts can be run in parallel without any fuss or degradation of performance.

Testing

Unit testing for Sky Cart is provided by Vitest, and is used to ensure that the library remains functional even as new changes are merged into future releases. Test cases written with Vitest are in the repo’s /test/ folder - check out cart.test.js to see an example of syntax used for testing.

Data layer

Prisma provides Object-Relation Mapping (ORM) for the data types used by Stripe Checkout for assembling a cart and the associated charges when users make a purchase. This again is a wonderfully simple implementation - you can see the entire object model for Sky Cart in schema.prisma.

Stripe Checkout

Sky Cart is used to coordinate and maintain a list of products to purchase from a Stripe Account. It provides APIs for users to add items to their cart, remove items, and update totals. Once users decide to make a purchase, a call to Sky Cart’s checkout API sends users to a Stripe’s hosted Checkout experience, where they can securely complete their purchase.

Making Sky Cart available as Open Source Software (OSS)

Josh to fill in this section - why make this project available as OSS?

Josh built Sky Cart to learn more about using Prisma & Vitest with SvelteKit. Since SvelteKit is still quite new, this project was a great way to dig a bit deeper into SvelteKit’s developer experience. By building a simple tool with SvelteKit, Josh was able to provide feedback for the SvelteKit team while creating some resources that would help others integrate Prisma with SvelteKit.

The project came around as the result of an idea Josh had for using Stripe to provide catalog and purchasing functionality for an ecommerce site. Because Stripe manages product & pricing data as well as the checkout process, it made sense to experiment with adding a cart between those two resources.

During the process of building Sky Cart, Josh identified and built a few open source utilities, which are published and available as NPM packages:

If you’re building with SvelteKit, check out these OSS resources as well:

Contributions Welcome

Since Sky Cart is completely open source, you can propose new features, add new functionality, or help maintain it by heading to GitHub in the repo joshnuss/sky-cart.

Give it a try!

And that’s it! Sky Cart is available for use straight away. You can add it to your site today by following the instructions in the README.md for Sky Cart. Make sure to follow Joshua Nussbaum on twitter @joshnuss, too!
item.author: irreverentmike

item.title: 我使用 t3-stack (Next.js) 创建了一个全栈且类型安全的个人网站！ | I made a full-stack and type-safe personal site using the t3-stack (Next.js)!
item.description:
首先，非常感谢 Dev.to 上的 1000 多名追随者，我真的不敢相信我们已经走到了这一步。 我什至没有想到我会走到这一步。 非常感谢我的所有关注者和所有阅读我内容的人，爱你们！

如果您是 Web 开发人员，并且每个开发人员都有自己独特的方式来使用独特的框架，那么您必须在某些时候构建投资组合网站或个人网站。 但是当涉及到投资组合网站时，它们中的大多数只是专注于前端，很少或没有后端，但对我来说并非如此。

我是create-t3-app的忠实粉丝，我也是它的 repo 的维护者。 这个博客不是教程，更像是我个人网站的演练和突破。

最初的想法

我最初的想法是一个非常小的站点，带有一个看起来更像侧边栏的最小导航栏，并且该设计在某种程度上受到了leerob 的站点的启发。 除此之外，我还希望我的网站能够显示一些很酷的动态和实时数据，例如我的 Spotify 数据、从dev.to获取的博客文章以及人们可以为我留言的留言簿！

注意：如果您不想读到最后，可以访问此处的站点并在此处查看源代码。 如果你喜欢这个项目，别忘了给项目打个星！

堆栈

正如我已经说过的，我在我的网站上使用了 t3-stack，它基本上由 -

  • 造型顺风
  • 用于类型安全 API 的 TRPC
  • 用于身份验证的 Next-Auth

你会惊讶地发现我在我的个人网站中使用了所有这些令人惊叹的技术！

主页

在构建 Web 应用程序时，我总是做的第一件事就是让它成为索引或主页。 我已经在上面提到了我希望我的导航栏看起来像什么，并且我的设计灵感来自 leerob.io。 现在，是时候实施它了。 在花费大量时间使用 Tailwind CSS 破坏和构建东西之后，我终于制作了这个我非常喜欢的索引页面 -

移动版的主页看起来有点不同 -

静态页面

现在我想要构建的下一个东西是静态页面，例如/about 、 /now和/links 。 第一个听起来很清楚，它是关于我的页面。 前两个时代的内容非常重，所以我决定使用 Markdown 和Tailwind 的排版插件来进行样式设置。 我还使用showdown来获取 markdown 并将其转换为 HTML。 上面的代码可以在该站点的 GitHub 存储库中找到。

链接页面是使用map()函数从一个对象生成的，该对象包含有关所有链接的信息，看起来像这样 -

 export type Link = { name : string ; url : string ; value : string ; icon : IconType ; }; export type Links = Link []; export const links : Links = [ { name : " Discord " , url : " https://discord.com/users/784363251940458516 " , value : " asheeshh#4872 " , icon : SiDiscord , }, { name : " GitHub " , url : " https://github.com/asrvd " , value : " @asrvd " , icon : SiGithub , }, ... ]

注意一切都是类型安全的，这是 Web 开发人员的最佳实践之一。 类型安全不应该是可选的。 好的部分从这里开始，Spotify 统计页面是使用获取我的 Spotify 数据创建的，例如从last.fm API 获取的当前播放歌曲、顶级艺术家和热门曲目。 我使用 SWR 进行数据获取，这使得构建类型安全的数据获取变得非常容易。

我有 3 个不同的 API 路由，它们每个都过滤掉响应，这样我就只能得到我正在使用的东西，这也有助于使其类型安全更容易。 页面实时更新。 这是页面的样子 -

留言簿

这是我个人网站中我最喜欢的部分，我真的很喜欢这个概念。 这个想法来自leerob 的留言簿。 我想构建类似的东西，人们可以在网站上为我和其他人留言。

这就是数据库、TRPC、身份验证和 Prisma 等后端东西的来源。 我为我的数据库使用了 PlanetScale 的免费套餐。 整个后端是使用 TRPC 和 Prisma 构建的，它们都负责类型安全并具有绝对惊人的 DX。

基本上，每当有人访问留言簿页面时，他们都可以看到所有以前发布的消息，但在登录之前他们无法发布消息。Next-Auth 使得在 Next.js 中实现身份验证变得非常容易。 登录后，您可以发送一条最多 100 字的短信，该短信使用Zod进行验证。

前往留言簿，在此处发送您自己的信息。 这是页面的样子 -

我还在我的网站上添加了一个 Cmd+K 界面，以使无鼠标导航更容易。 它使用kbar和使用 Tailwind CSS 的样式制作。 这是它的样子 -

结论

总的来说，我真的很喜欢这个网站的最终外观，我也计划在网站上添加更多页面和很酷的功能！ 如果您正在寻找使用 vercel @ asrvd.me部署的站点，并且源代码可在GitHub Repo上找到。

感谢阅读，下一篇博客再见！



原文:

First of all, thanks a lot for 1000+ followers on Dev.to, I really can’t believe we’ve come this far. I hadn’t even thought that I would make it this far. Thank you so much to all my followers and everyone who reads my content, love you all!

A portfolio site or a personal site is something that you have to build at some point for sure if you’re a web developer and every developer has their own unique way of doing it using unique frameworks. But when it comes to portfolio sites, most of them are just frontend focused with little or no backend but that was not the case for me.

Next.js is always my first choice for building web apps but I wanted a type-safe and full-stack solution for my site, so I decided to use the t3-stack! I’m a big fan of create-t3-app and I’m also a maintainer for it’s repo. This blog is not a tutorial but more like a walkthrough and breakthrough of my personal site.

The Initial Idea

The initial idea I had in my mind was a very minimal site with a minimal navbar that would look more like a sidebar, and the design would be somewhat inspired by leerob’s site. Apart from this, I also wanted my site to display some cool dynamic and real-time data like my Spotify data, my blog posts fetched from dev.to and a guestbook where people could leave messages for me!

NOTE: In case you don’t want to read till end, you can visit the site here and view the source code here. Don’t forget to give a star to the project if you liked it!

The Stack

As I already said, I used the t3-stack for my site which is basically made up of -

  • Next.js as a framework
  • Tailwind for styling
  • Prisma as ORM
  • TRPC for type-safe APIs
  • Next-Auth for authentication

And you’ll be surprised to know that I have used all of these pieces of amazing tech in my personal site!

The Home Page

The first thing that I always work on while building a web app is making it’s index or home page. I already mentioned above what I wanted my Nav Bar to look like and that my design was inspired by leerob.io. Now, it was time to implement it. After spending a lot of hours breaking and building things using Tailwind CSS, I finally made this index page which I love a lot -

The Home Page looks a bit different on mobile version -

The Static Pages

Now the next things I wanted to build were the static pages like /about, /now, and /links. The first one sounds pretty clear that it’s the About Me page. /now has info about what I’m doing these days and /links has all my links listed in one single page.

The first two ages are very heavy on content so I decided to use markdown and tailwind’s typography plugin for styling. I also used showdown to fetch the markdown and turn it into HTML. The code for the above can be found on the site’s GitHub repository.

The links page is generated using the map() function from an object containing info about all the links which looks something like this -

export type Link = { name: string; url: string; value: string; icon: IconType;
};
export type Links = Link[]; export const links: Links = [ { name: "Discord", url: "https://discord.com/users/784363251940458516", value: "asheeshh#4872", icon: SiDiscord, }, { name: "GitHub", url: "https://github.com/asrvd", value: "@asrvd", icon: SiGithub, }, ...
]

Notice how everything is type-safe, that’s one of the best practices for web developers. Type Safety shouldn’t be optional. IconType is imported from the react-icons library.

The Spotify Stats Page

Here starts the good part, the Spotify stats page is made using fetching my Spotify data like - Currently Playing Song, Top Artists, and Top Tracks which are fetched from last.fm API. I have used SWR for data fetching which makes building type safe data fetching very easy.

I have 3 different API routes for each of them which filter out the response so that I only get what I’m using which also helps making it type-safe easier. The page updates in real time. Here is what the page looks like -

The GuestBook

This is my favorite part of my personal site and I really like the concept. The idea was taken from leerob’s guestbook. I wanted to build something similar where people can leave messages on the site for me and others.

This is where backend stuff comes in like database, TRPC, authentication, and Prisma. I used PlanetScale’s free tier for my database. The whole backend is built using TRPC and Prisma, both of which take care of type-safety and have absolutely amazing DX.

Basically, whenever someone visits the guestbook page, they can see all the previously posted messages but they can’t post a message till they are signed in. Next-Auth makes it very easy to implement auth in Next.js. Once you’re signed in you can send a text message of maximum 100 words which is validates using Zod.

Head over to the guestbook to send your own message here. Here is what the page looks like -

Cmd+K Interface

I also added a Cmd+K interface to my site to make mouse-less navigation easier. It’s made using kbar and styles using Tailwind CSS. Here is what it looks like -

Conclusion

Overall, I really like how the site finally looks and I’m planning to add more pages and cool features to the site too! In case you’re looking for the site it’s deployed using vercel @ asrvd.me, and the source code is available on the GitHub Repo.

Thanks for reading, see you in the next blog!

thedailyeng

item.title: 9 个 Shopify 应用集成以创建终极商店 | 9 Shopify App Integrations to Create the Ultimate Store
item.description:
首先，您要解决哪个业务问题？ 您是否需要更好的分析和报告，或者您是否正在尝试提高您的商店转化率？ 在找出您的关键问题后，您可以前往 Google 搜索或 Shopify 应用商店。 你从哪里开始看？ 假设您找到了一组您感兴趣的应用程序。 你怎么知道哪一个适合你？ 您是否查看评论和评级、产品截图、计划和定价，或者是否有免费试用？ 您可以花一整天时间为您的商店浏览应用程序。 所以我们是来帮忙的。

以下是 9 个 Shopify 应用程序，它们可以完成从提高商店转化率到按需打印的所有工作。 此列表适用于每个 Shopify 企业主。

集成类型：业务分析

这个应用程序有什么作用？ 想象一下，当您不再需要单独登录每个分析帐户时，您将有多少时间回到您的一天。 您将在一个集中的位置拥有最有价值的情报，您可以在任何地方访问和比较。

您将一目了然地了解您的广告的效果、您的电子邮件是否产生了收入、您的净利润、ROAS 等等。

客户怎么看？ 两个突出的评论包括查看和理解 Triple Whale 中的所有数据是多么容易。 人们说分析和见解非常详细，您可以根据自己的指标自信地做出决策。

至于一些缺点，同样的评论者还提到数据有时会加载缓慢。 其他人希望能够将他们的数据导出为 PDF 表单。

免费试用还是免费增值？ 没有免费试用或免费增值计划。 起价为 100 美元/月。 访问Triple Whale 的定价和计划页面了解更多信息。

集成类型：潜在客户捕获、电子邮件和 SMS 营销

这个应用程序有什么作用？ 因此，您将能够在一个空间中建立您的电子邮件列表、保存废弃的购物车、发送赚钱的电子邮件和文本。

创建精美的电子邮件并将您的设计保存为模板，以备日后使用。 如果您不想从头开始，您可以从数十种预先构建的电子邮件和弹出模板中进行选择，并添加您自己的风格。

在几秒钟内将产品从您的商店拉到您的电子邮件中。 在 Privy 中创建自动同步到您的 Shopify 帐户的优惠券代码。 在弹出窗口、电子邮件和文本中轻松使用这些优惠券。

客户怎么看？ 根据 G2 上的 118 条评论，Privy 获得4.6 星评级。 根据Shopify 上超过 24,500 条评论，它还获得 4.6 星评级，使其成为 Shopify 应用商店中评论最多的销售应用。

一位客户报告说，他们开始使用 Privy 弹出窗口来捕获潜在客户，然后转向自动化、购物车放弃活动和每周电子邮件活动，因为他们非常喜欢它。

许多用户称赞 Privy 的电子邮件捕获表单在移动和桌面上的外观。 他们喜欢快速构建自定义表单，当网站访问者也执行某些行为时触发。 一些人认为 Privy 的客户服务非常出色。

关于不喜欢，一些评论者希望有更多关于 Privy 的报道。 一些审阅者也希望能够从他们的电子邮件捕获中自动导出数据。

免费试用还是免费增值？ 之后，它的起价为每月 15 美元。 访问Privy 的定价和计划页面了解更多信息。

集成类型：产品评论、客户推荐

这个应用程序有什么作用？ 在他们的帮助下，您可以轻松地自动化您的评论流程并通过鼓励他们放弃对他们购买的评论来吸引您的最佳客户。 这些可以建立对您品牌的信任，以刺激更多销售。 您还可以激励客户使用他们的推荐工具以您的方式发送他们的朋友和家人。

客户怎么看？ 近 10,000 家商家对 Loox 进行了评级和评论。

客户喜欢 Loox 的设置和使用简单明了。 许多人称它为收集评论的好工具，因为它很容易导入并将它们与其他应用程序集成。 他们的客服团队也获得了很高的评价。

至于缺点，一些客户希望能够编辑评论，例如客户想稍后添加内容或更改照片。 其他人提到事情有时会变得有点过于混乱。

免费试用还是免费增值？ 之后，它的起价为每月 9.99 美元。 访问Loox 的定价和计划页面了解更多信息。

集成类型：按需打印 托运和仓储

这个应用程序有什么作用？ 使用 Printful，您可以在线创建和销售您的定制产品、打印并按需发货。 告别持有昂贵的库存，向更好的商品销售方式问好。

无论您是为公司业务创建定制咖啡杯，还是作为个体企业家在线销售 T 恤，Printful 都会在整个履行过程中照顾客户的订单。

客户怎么看？ 客户发现 Printful 易于使用并欣赏他们的资源库以开始按需打印。 客户也对 POD 产品的质量和 Printful 的客户支持聊天给予了高度评价。

根据其 G2 概况，相对较高的运费似乎是客户最常见的抱怨。

免费试用还是免费增值？ 之后，您可以进入免费增值计划，客户只需在下订单后为他们购买或出售的产品付费。 付费计划低至每月 9 美元。 访问Printful 的定价和计划页面了解更多信息。

整合类型：忠诚度积分、奖励计划

这个应用程序有什么作用？ 奖励、积分、折扣等可以帮助您将新客户转变为终身购物者，他们乐于向他们认识的每个人宣传您的品牌。 此外，您可以自定义您的忠诚度和奖励计划，以匹配您品牌的外观和感觉，以获得无缝美感。

由于 Smile.io 使购物体验如此互动，使用此应用程序的在线商店可能会看到回头客增加。

客户怎么看？ 它还在 Shopify 应用商店中获得4.8 星评级，拥有超过 4,700 条客户评论。

客户对易于搜索的简单界面赞不绝口。 许多人喜欢简单的积分系统和品牌和客户的用户友好界面。

客户很难说出任何投诉。 但是，一些人提到系统每隔一段时间就会出现故障。

免费试用还是免费增值？ 之后，起价为 49 美元/月。 访问他们的计划和定价页面了解更多信息。

集成类型：帮助台和实时聊天

这个应用程序有什么作用？ 在短短一小时内回答您收到的所有客户问题可能需要您的品牌日。 但是 Gorgias 应用程序将您的所有支持票保存在一个集中位置，因此您可以在几分钟内将它们淘汰。

您还可以在不离开帮助台的情况下编辑订单、修改订阅和退款。 客户怎么看？ 在 Shopify 应用商店中，Gorgias 获得了 500 多家商家的4.4 星评级。

根据对 G2 的评论，客户报告说 Gorgias 简单、易于使用且设计直观。 他们喜欢它如何帮助他们简化回复和自动化消息以节省时间。

另一方面，一些客户抱怨无法自定义他们的报告。 其他人希望有一个“下一步”按钮可以快速移动到下一张票。

免费试用还是免费增值？ 之后，它的起价为每月 10 美元，每张额外的票收取 0.40 美元的费用。 访问Gorgias 定价和计划页面了解更多信息。

整合类型：数字广告平台

这个应用程序有什么作用？ 直接从 AdRoll 发布您的展示广告、社交媒体广告和电子邮件，您将能够从一个方便的位置吸引现有客户、吸引新客户、推动转化等。

客户怎么看？ 它也是跨渠道广告软件的 2022 G2 类别领导者。 它还在 Shopify 应用商店中获得 4.2 星评级，有近 400 条客户评论。

根据客户意见：

  • 设置观众是一件轻而易举的事
  • 上传资产超级简单
  • 报告易于阅读
  • 您可以使用互动元素增强您的广告
  • 您可以使用人口统计和亲和力定位来吸引您的确切客户

其他人表示，他们的转化跟踪可能需要一些改进。

免费试用还是免费增值？ 之后，它的起价为每月 40 美元，并随您的每月唯一身份访问者数量而变化。 访问AdRoll 的定价和计划页面了解更多信息。

集成类型：登陆页面构建器

这个应用程序有什么作用？ 他们直观的拖放编辑器使构建和自定义登录页面变得轻而易举。 此外，您可以针对移动设备优化您的页面，以便用户可以在所有设备上享受无缝体验。

从针对转换和用户体验进行优化的预构建登录页面模板库中进行选择。 这些模板还与 Shopify 的最新商店主题无缝集成。 这对 Shopify 企业来说是双赢的。

客户怎么看？ 在 Shopify 应用商店中，它还根据超过 6,800 条客户评论获得4.9 星评级。

其他人则喜欢直观的构建器，它可以让您在没有专业开发人员的情况下快速设计令人惊叹的登录页面。 用户还欣赏 PageFly 的 24/7 客户支持。

至于负面因素，一位客户表示，有些任务执行起来可能比其他任务复杂一些，例如上传新字体，但仅此而已。

免费试用还是免费增值？ 之后，它的起价为每月 24 美元，并且每增加 10 个页槽就可以扩展一次。 访问PageFly 的定价和计划页面了解更多信息。

集成类型：结账时一键追加销售

这个应用程序有什么作用？ 结帐促销可用于在结帐阶段运行促销优惠和内容。 根据他们的网站，该应用程序帮助 Shopify 商家将其结账的转化率提高了 10%。

您甚至可以获得提供分层定价的能力，因此客户可以根据他们一次购买的数量选择不同的折扣。

客户怎么看？ 一位客户提到该应用程序运行良好，并且具有非常好的选择。 他们还给了他们的客户支持团队 10/10。 另一位用户喜欢出色的用户体验 (UX) 以及定制和测试报价的容易程度。

免费试用还是免费增值？ 之后，起价为 19 美元/月，超过 250 美元的订单每单 0.03 美元。访问结帐促销定价和计划页面了解更多信息。

就是这样，我们易于理解的 9 个 Shopify 应用程序集成列表可以让您更接近解决最紧迫的业务问题。

从此列表中选择一两个应用程序，甚至开始一些免费试用，看看它是否适合您的商店。 如果没有，您可以在 App Store 中查看大量其他应用程序。



原文:

With over 7,000 integrations in the Shopify App Store, it can be overwhelming trying to figure which apps are right for your store.

First, which business problem are you trying to solve? Do you need better analytics and reporting, or are your trying to improve your store conversion rates? There are never-ending problems that Shopify apps can address.

After figuring out your key problem, you take to Google Search or the Shopify App Store. Where do you begin to look? Shopify has a variety of Categories and Collections you can scour, and their main navigation sorts apps by business outcome (e.g. Sell more stuff, Customize your store, and so on).

Let's say you find a group of apps that have your interest. How do you know which one is right for you? Do you look at reviews and ratings, product screenshots, plans and pricing, or if there's a free trial? You can spend an entire day combing through apps for your store. So we're here to help.

Below are 9 Shopify apps that can do everything from boost your store conversion rate to print-on-demand. This list has something for every Shopify business owner.

1. Triple Whale

Type of integration: Business Analytics

What does this app do? Triple Whale aggregates all the metrics you use throughout your marketing campaigns — from Facebook and Pinterest ads to email, Shopify sales, and more — and displays them in one easy-to-use dashboard.

Imagine how much time you’ll get back in your day when you no longer have to log into each analytics account individually. You’ll have your most valuable intel in one centralized location you can access and compare anywhere.

You’ll see how your ads are performing, whether your emails are generating revenue, your net profit, ROAS, etc., at a quick yet informative glance.

What do customers think? Triple Whale has a 4.6-star rating on G2 with many positive reviews.

Two standout comments include how easy it is to view and understand all your data in Triple Whale. People say the analytics and insights are so detailed that you can feel confident making decisions based on your metrics.

As for some cons, the same reviewers also mentioned that the data can load slowly at times. Others would like the ability to export their data to a PDF form.

Free trial or Freemium? No free trial or freemium plan. It starts at $100/month. Visit Triple Whale's pricing & plan page for more information.

2. Privy

Type of integration: Lead Capture, Email, & SMS Marketing

What does this app do? Privy offers a quick way to grow your online sale with email and SMS marketing.

Privy provides all the email, conversion, and SMS tools you’ll need to drive sales at every stage of a customer's journey. So you’ll be able to build your email list, save abandoned carts, send money-making emails and texts, all in one space.

The Privy app includes an easy to use drag and drop email editor. Create stunning emails and save your designs as templates that you can use in the future. If you don't want to start from scratch, you can choose from dozens of pre-built email and popup templates and add your own touch.

Privy has a powerful Shopify integration. Pull products from your store into your emails in seconds. Create coupon codes in Privy that automatically sync to your Shopify account. Easily use these coupons across popups, emails, and texts.

What do customers think? Privy boasts a 4.6-star rating based on 118 reviews on G2. It also has a 4.6-star rating based on over 24,500 reviews on Shopify, making it the most reviewed sales app in the Shopify App Store.

One customer reported that they started using Privy popups to capture leads and then moved on to automations, cart abandonment campaigns, and weekly email campaigns because they loved it so much.

Many users praise the look of Privy’s email capture forms on both mobile and desktop. They enjoy how quickly they can build custom forms that trigger when website visitors perform certain behaviors as well. Several rated Privy’s customer service as excellent.

Regarding dislikes, some reviewers wish there was more reporting with Privy. A few reviewers would like the ability to automatically export the data from their email captures too.

Free trial or Freemium? Privy offers a 15-day free trial and a freemium plan for <101 email contacts. After that, it starts at $15/month. Visit Privy's pricing & plan page for more information.

3. Loox

Type of Integration: Product Reviews, Customer Referrals

What does this app do? Loox is a review app that allows your customers to share their experiences with your brand using visual reviews and referrals.

With their help, you can easily automate your review process and engage your best customers by encouraging them to drop a review about their purchases. These build trust in your brand to spur more sales. You can also incentivize customers to send their friends and family your way with their referral tool.

What do customers think? Loox has a 4.9-star rating on both G2 and the Shopify App Store. Almost 10,000 merchants have rated and reviewed Loox.

Customers love how simple and straightforward Loox is to set up and use. Many call it a great tool for collecting reviews because it’s so easy to import and integrate them with other apps. Their customer service team also scored high praise.

As for the downsides, some customers would like the ability to edit reviews, like if a customer would like to add something or change a photo later. Others mentioned that things can get a little too cluttered at times.

Free trial or Freemium? Loox offers a 14-day free trial. After that, it starts at $9.99/month. Visit Loox's pricing & plan page for more information.

4. Printful

Type of Integration: Print On Demand Drop Shipping & Warehousing

What does this app do? Printful helps you turn your ideas into premium products without worrying about inventory and shipping.

Using Printful, you can create and sell your custom products online, print, and ship them on demand. Say goodbye to holding expensive inventory and hello to a better way to sell your merch.

Whether you're creating custom coffee mugs for your corporate business or selling t-shirts online as a solopreneur, Printful takes care of your customers' orders throughout the entire fulfillment process.

What do customers think? Printful has a 4.6-star rating on the Shopify App Store with over 4,000 customer reviews.

Customers find Printful easy to use and appreciate their library of resources to get started with print on demand. Customers also give high marks to the quality of POD products and Printful’s customer support chat.

Relatively high shipping prices seems to be the most common complaint from customers, according to its G2 profile.

Free trial or Freemium? Printful offers a 14-day free trial. After that, you can enter a freemium plan where customers pay only for the products they buy or sell after an order is made. Paid plans start as low as $9/month. Visit Printful's pricing & plan page for more information.

5. Smile.io

Type of Integration: Loyalty Points, Rewards Programs

What does this app do? Smile.io is a loyalty and rewards program app that lets customers rack up points for engaging with your brand.

Rewards, points, discounts, and more can help you turn new customers into lifelong shoppers who happily advocate for your brand to everyone they know. Plus, you can customize your loyalty and rewards program to match the look and feel of your brand for a seamless aesthetic.

Because Smile.io makes the shopping experience so interactive, online stores that utilize this app could see an uptick in repeat customers.

What do customers think? Smile.io currently holds a 4-star rating on G2. It also has a 4.8-star rating on the Shopify App Store with over 4,700 customer reviews.

Customers rave about the simple interface that’s also easily searchable. Many love the straightforward points system and the user-friendly interface for brands and customers alike.

Customers were hard-pressed to name any complaints. However, a few mentioned that the system goes down every once in a while.

Free trial or Freemium? Smile.io offers a free plan for up to 200 monthly orders through the app. After that, it starts $49/month. Visit their plan & pricing page for more information.

6. Gorgias

Type of Integration: Helpdesk and Live Chat

What does this app do? Gorgias is an ecommerce helpdesk that may transform your customer service and turn this area of your business into an epic profit center.

It might take your brand days to answer all the customer questions you receive in just one hour. But the Gorgias app keeps all your support tickets in one centralized location, so you can knock them out in a matter of minutes.

You’ll also be able to edit orders, modify subscriptions, and refund payments without ever leaving the helpdesk. Gorgias could be the only app you need to achieve exceptional customer service.

What do customers think? Gorgias boasts an impressive 4.5-stars based on 480+ reviews on G2. On the Shopify App Store, Gorgias has a 4.4-star rating from over 500 merchants.

According to its reviews on G2, customer report that Gorgias is straightforward, easy to use, and intuitively designed. They love how it helps them streamline replies and automate messages to save time.

On the other hand, some customers complained about not being able to customize their reports. Others wish there was a “next” button to quickly move to the following ticket.

Free trial or Freemium? Gorgias offers a 7-day free trial. After that, it starts at $10/month with a $0.40 charge per each additional ticket. Visit the Gorgias pricing & plan page for more information.

7. AdRoll

Type of Integration: Digital Advertising Platform

What does this app do? AdRoll helps you pool all your advertising campaigns — across 500 platforms — into one place, so you can streamline your marketing efforts and save time.

Launch your display ads, social media ads, and emails right from AdRoll, and you’ll be able to engage your existing customers, attract new ones, drive conversions, and more from one convenient location.

AdRoll's platform is powered by over 15 years of data from hundreds of thousands of brands, and even more shoppers – allowing its customers to make more informed decisions for their digital ads.

What do customers think? AdRoll has over 500 reviews on G2 and with a 4-star rating. It is also a 2022 G2 category leader for cross-channel advertising software. It also has a 4.2-star rating on the Shopify App Store with almost 400 customer reviews.

According to customer comments:

  • Setting up audiences is a breeze
  • Uploading assets is super simple
  • Reporting is easy to read
  • You can enhance your ads with interactive elements
  • You can use demographic and affinity targeting to reach your exact customers

Some on G2 people did mention that the cost to use AdRoll is higher than similar competitors. Others said their conversion tracking could use some improvement.

Free trial or Freemium? AdRoll offers a 30-day free trial and a free-to-install plan based on your advertising budget. After that, it starts at $40/month and scales with your Monthly Unique Visitor count. Visit AdRoll's pricing & plan page for more information.

8. PageFly

Type of Integration: Landing Page Builder

What does this app do? PageFly is Shopify’s number one page builder, snagging over 150,000 active users.

Their intuitive drag-and-drop editor makes building and customizing your landing pages a piece of cake. Plus, you can optimize your pages for mobile so users can enjoy a seamless experience across all their devices.

Choose from a library of pre-built landing page templates that are optimized for conversion and user experience. These templates also integrate seamlessly with Shopify's latest store themes. It's a win-win for Shopify businesses.

What do customers think? PageFly has a 4.9-star rating on G2. On the Shopify App Store, it also has a 4.9-star rating based on over 6,800 customer reviews.

Customers on G2 mentioned that the page builder is super easy to learn and integrates perfectly with their Shopify stores. Others love the intuitive builder that lets you quickly design stunning landing pages without an expert developer on hand. Users also appreciate PageFly’s 24/7 customer support.

As for the negatives, one customer said some tasks can be a little more complicated than others to perform, such as uploading a new font, but that was about it.

Free trial or Freemium? PageFly offers a freemium plan for one page slot. After that, it starts at $24/month and scales every additional 10 page slots. Visit PageFly's pricing & plan page for more information.

9. Checkout Promotions

Type of Integration: One-click Upsells at Checkout

What does this app do? Checkout Promotions can be used to run promotional offers and content at your checkout stage. According to their website, the app helps Shopify merchants convert 10% more of their checkouts.

Checkout Promotions can offer your customers impulse purchases and upsells and even be used to send free samples – whatever offer your store decides to setup, this app has the flexibility. You’ll even score the ability to offer tiered pricing, so customers choose different discounts based on the amount they buy at once.

What do customers think? Checkout Promotions has an impressive perfect 5-star rating based on 40+ reviews in the Shopify App Store.

One customer mentioned that the app works beautifully and has really great options. They also gave their customer support team a 10/10. Another user loved the excellent user experience (UX) and how easy it is to customize and test offers.

Free trial or Freemium? Checkout Promotions offers a 7-day free trial and a freemium plan for up to 100 monthly orders. After that, it starts at $19/month and scales $0.03 per order above 250. Visit Checkout Promotions pricing & plan page for more information.

That's it, our easy to understand list of 9 Shopify app integrations that can get you one step closer to solving your most pressing business problems.

Choose one or two apps from this list, and even start some free trials to see if it's a right fit for your store. If not, there's tons of other apps in the App Store that you can check out.
item.author: devinpickell

item.author: victorarana

item.title: FileKinds™ 提案、文件和文件夹规则 | FileKinds™ Proposal, rules for files and folders
item.description:
重点是什么？ 许多 JavaScript / TypeScript 框架需要从文件中导出专门的变量。 例如，在Next.js中，我们有具有特定类型的getServerSideProps ，在 Deno Fresh 中，我们有具有特定类型的handlers 。 通常这些文件要求默认导出也是组件类型。

随着元框架的兴起，服务器+客户端代码的结合变得越来越流行。 很明显，服务器+客户端的结合只会变得越来越复杂。 目前有几种方法可以编写拆分代码：

  1. 单独的文件，如thing.client.js和thing.server.js
  2. 使用 AST server和client命名导出和拆分的一个文件
  3. 使用 AST 中的注释来定义应该拆分的// @server & // @client
  4. 来自@reggi/universal universal包的命名导出可以由您选择的 AST 解析器跟踪，您可以将函数传递给通用并在函数参数中拆分代码

所有这些解决方案都增加了如何创建文件/文件夹的复杂性，并且对于如何做到这一点有明确的规则对于下一波框架的启动至关重要。

对于文件组呢？ 我总是在 mac 生态系统中发现.app扩展名被应用到文件夹并抽象出具有可执行性的文件夹内容（例如，当您单击它时）很有趣。 我喜欢包含服务器功能的包含组件的想法，该功能也可以执行类似的操作。 通过给文件夹一个扩展，元²框架可以在没有任何配置的情况下了解该组件的工作方式。 例如，对于反应服务器组件，我们可以使用rsc扩展，也可以使用它自己的扩展等。

 about.rsc server.tsx client.tsx

目标

  1. 为具有特定导出签名的文件/文件组提供 linting。
  2. 提供机会，然后允许元框架允许元框架从任何元框架呈现服务器 + 组件代码。 示例 Deno Fresh 路由

    作为一个例子，我选择了新鲜的 Deno，因为它对路由使用命名导出。 使用 eslint，可以创建一个自定义规则来限制可以从此 FileKind™ 进行哪些导出，例如，如果为handler导出分配了JSX.Element而不是它需要的对象，它可能会给出 linting 错误。 我们可以创建一个名为deno-fresh-route的新 FileKind™。

    示例 Deno Fresh 路由

    // routes/about.tsx import { Handlers } from " $fresh/server.ts " ; export const handler : Handlers = { async GET ( req , ctx ) { const resp = await ctx . render (); resp . headers . set ( " X-Custom-Header " , " Hello " ); return resp ; }, }; export default function AboutPage () { return ( < h1 > About </ h1 > < p > This is the about page. </ p > ); }

    第 2 部分：执法

    当您拥有像deno-fresh-route这样的自定义 FileKind™ 时，您可以用它做什么？ 你如何执行/实施它？ 以下是人们应该能够使用它的两种方式。

    方法一“扩展”

    注册deno-fresh-route时，将包含一个 eslint 包。 我喜欢 FileKinds™s 能够声明像about.fsh.ts这样的子扩展的想法

    方法二“规则”

    可以理解，库作者可能不想实现文件子扩展。 他们可能只想声明给定文件夹中的所有文件都说routes属于某种类型。 这应该可以在框架/编辑器级别进行配置，并且用户不会真正看到。 不幸的是，我想不出比路由级别的filekinds.config.json更好的方法来告诉编辑器和 linter 哪些规则应该针对哪些文件/文件夹运行。

    方法三“评论”

    类似于我们目前处理 JSX 运行时的方式，您可以使用文件顶部的注释来定义其 FileType™️。

    提案文件kinds.config.json

     { "routes/*" : "filekind.deno-fresh-route"
}

    第 3 部分：组织

    该项目的一部分是创建一个标准机构来声明特定的文件扩展名类型，例如about.rsc ，以及一个可以检测这些文件类型并将它们自动带入您的项目的 vscode 扩展名（通过请求访问）。 现在，为了实现这种级别的 linting，每个框架都需要实现自己的 vscode 插件。 我认为如果我们可以拥有一个所有框架都可以使用的工具，那将是非常有趣的。

    单个文件的提案注册表定义

    { // name of the FileKind™ separate from the eslint-package-name "name" : "deno-fresh-route" , // extension of eslint rules with npm modules and options "rules" : [ "deno-fresh-route" ], // reserved extension "extension" : "{name}.fsh.ts"
}

    文件夹的提案注册表定义

    { // name of the FileKind™ separate from the eslint-package-name "name" : "sveltekit-page" , // extension of eslint rules with npm modules and options "folder" : { "+page.svelte" : { // possibly nothing not sure what to do with non js / ts files // if you really wanted you may need to use vite to compile then // run the rule checker "rules" : [ "svelte" ] }, "+page.js" : { "rules" : [ "svelte-page-js" ] }, "+page.server.js" : { "rules" : [ "svelte-page-server" ] }, // reserved extension "extension" : "{name}.page.sveltekit"
}

    可能🚩

    1. 人们不关心文件/文件夹掉毛和开销
    2. 人们讨厌像about.denofreshpreactroute.ts这样的长文件扩展名
    3. 人们讨厌像about.dfpr.ts这样的抽象文件扩展名
    4. 版本控制 FileKinds™
    5. 争夺好的简短有用的扩展名。 在一个框架中包含多个元框架（ qwik 、 solidstart 、 sveltekit 、 next ）实际上并不可行。
    6. 作为一种实践，在一个 FileKind™ 中包含组件 + 服务器代码并不是真正需要的。
    7. 对其他危险信号开放
    8. 就像 babel 再次不得不决定什么代码在哪里就像语法插件一样😵‍💫

    想象一个可以容纳 Web 套接字操作端点、可从服务器获得的 tree-shaken 客户端导入和服务器端点的单个文件。 这是一个服务器 FileKind，允许您为不同类型的可调用交互定义导出。 参数抖动示例：

    一个不同的静态服务器生成示例不是来自导出，而是来自特定模块universal声明的关键字，它将构成服务器端点。 我在一个 astro 文件中执行此操作，这会使事情变得更加困难，但这个想法就在那里。 使用 web-component 来提供全局变量。

     --- import { staticallyParsedRules , universal } from ' @reggi/example ' universal (() => ({ thing : { server : () => { console . log ( ' hi from server ' ) }, client : () => { console . log ( ' hi from client ' ) }, socket : () => { console . log ( ' hi from server socket ' ) } } })) --- < dom - global - rules rules = { staticallyParsedRules } >< /dom-global-rules > < div onclick = " thing.server() " > log something on the server < /div > < div onclick = " thing.socket() " > log something on the socket < /div > < div onclick = " thing.client() " > log something on the client < /div >


原文:

Whats the point? Many JavaScript / TypeScript frameworks require specialized variables to be exported from files. For instance within Next.js we have getServerSideProps which has a specific type, within Deno Fresh we have handlers which has a specific type. Usually these files require the default export to also be a component type.

With the rise of meta-frameworks the union of server + client side code becoming increasingly popular. Frameworks like Next.js popularized named exports like getServerSideProps in a file that did specific things within the framework. It's becoming apparent that the union of server + client is only going to become increasingly complex. Currently there are a couple ways to author split code:

  1. separate files like thing.client.js and thing.server.js
  2. one file where you have named exports and split using the AST server & client
  3. use a comment in the AST to define which should be split // @server & // @client
  4. a named export from a package say universal from @reggi/universal can be tracked by your AST parser of choice and you can pass a function into universal and split the code within the function argument

All of these solutions add complexity for how files / folders need to be authored and having clear rules around how to do that will be essential in getting this next wave of frameworks off the ground.

What about for groups of files? I always found it interesting within the mac ecosystem that the .app extension was applied to folders and abstracted out the contents of the folder with executability (when you click on it for instance). I like the idea of a contained component that has server functionality performing something like this as well. By giving the folder an extension a meta²-framework could pickup on how that component works without any configuration. For example for react server components we could use the rsc extension could use its own extension, etc.

about.rsc
  server.tsx
  client.tsx

Goals

  1. To provide linting for files / groups of files that have specific export signatures.
  2. To (optionally?) provide plugins on how a server should handle these files in a server framework like hattip

Provide the opportunity to then allow meta-frameworks to allow meta²-frameworks to render server + component code from any meta-framework. (allow a framework to potentially render out about.rsc and about.solid on the same server, potentially something like astro or another project could do this.

Example Deno Fresh Route

As an example I picked Deno fresh, because it uses named exports for routes. A route in Deno requires a default function to return that returns the component (in this case preact) and a handler object. With eslint it is possible to create a custom rule that would restrict what exports can be made from this FileKind™, for instance it could give a linting error if the handler export was assigned a JSX.Element instead of an object like it requires. We could create a new FileKind™ called deno-fresh-route.

Example Deno Fresh Route

// routes/about.tsx import { Handlers } from "$fresh/server.ts"; export const handler: Handlers = { async GET(req, ctx) { const resp = await ctx.render(); resp.headers.set("X-Custom-Header", "Hello"); return resp; },
}; export default function AboutPage() { return ( <h1>About</h1> <p>This is the about page.</p> );
}

Part 2: Enforcement

When you have a custom FileKind™ like deno-fresh-route what can you do with it? How do you enforce / implement it? Here are two ways people should be able to use it.

Method one "Extensions"

When deno-fresh-route is registered a eslint package is included. I like the idea of FileKinds™s being able declare a sub-extension like about.fsh.ts

Method two "Rules"

Understandably library authors may not want to implement file sub-extensions. They may just want to state that all files within a given folder say routes are of a certain type. This should be configurable at the framework / editor level and not really seen by users. Unfortunately I can't think of a better way of doing this then a filekinds.config.json at the route level something to tell the editor and linter which rules should run for which files / folders.

Method three "Comments"

Similar to how we currently juggle JSX runtimes you could use a comment at the top of a file to define its FileType™️.

Proposal filekinds.config.json

{ "routes/*": "filekind.deno-fresh-route"
}

Part 3: The Organization

Part of this project is to create a standards body to claim specific file extension types for example about.rsc, along with a vscode extension that can detect these file types and bring them into your project automatically (by requesting access). Right now in order to achieve this level of linting every framework needs to implement their own vscode plugin. I think it would be really interesting if we could just have one that all frameworks could tap into.

Proposal Registry Definition for single file

{ // name of the FileKind™ separate from the eslint-package-name "name": "deno-fresh-route", // extension of eslint rules with npm modules and options "rules": ["deno-fresh-route"], // reserved extension "extension": "{name}.fsh.ts"
}

Proposal Registry Definition for Folder

{ // name of the FileKind™ separate from the eslint-package-name "name": "sveltekit-page", // extension of eslint rules with npm modules and options "folder": { "+page.svelte": { // possibly nothing not sure what to do with non js / ts files // if you really wanted you may need to use vite to compile then // run the rule checker "rules": ["svelte"] }, "+page.js": { "rules": ["svelte-page-js"] }, "+page.server.js": { "rules": ["svelte-page-server"] }, // reserved extension "extension": "{name}.page.sveltekit"
}

Possible 🚩

  1. People don't care about file / folder linting and the overhead
  2. People hate long file extensions like about.denofreshpreactroute.ts
  3. People hate abstract file extensions like about.dfpr.ts
  4. Versioning FileKinds™
  5. Fights over good short useful extension names. (and the problems that come with governance)
  6. It's not really feasible to include a multiple meta-frameworks (qwik, solidstart, sveltekit, next) in one framework.
  7. Including component + server code in one FileKind™ is not really desired overall as a practice.
  8. Open to other red-flags
  9. Security issues with the mono vscode extension
  10. It's like babel all over again having to decide what code lives where is like syntax plugins 😵‍💫

Imagine a single file that can house web sockets action endpoints, tree-shaken client imports available from the server, and server endpoints. This is a Server FileKind that allows you define exports for different types of callable interactions. The AST tool would parse this file and shake out each part into server handlers using something like hattip inspired by this discussion here in brillout/vite-plugin-ssr.

export const socketActions = { hello () { // web socket action endpoint (like liveview) console.log('hello on server'); return { hello: 'true'} }
} export const clientImports = { hello () { // this is a chunk of js that will be shaken out of this file (with it's external scopes if any?) // imported into the client not ajax // this isn't loaded at runtime and is loaded on a dom event (like qwick) console.log('hello on client'); return { hello: 'true'} }
} export const serverEndpoints = { hello () { // this is a server endpoint (like telefunc) console.log('hello on server'); return { hello: 'true'} }
}

Argument shaking example:

A different example of static server generation not from exports but from a keyword from a specific module universal declarations would compose the server endpoints. I did this in an astro file which would make things extra hard, but the idea is there. Uses web-component to provide globals.

---
import { staticallyParsedRules, universal } from '@reggi/example' universal(() => ({ thing: { server: () => { console.log('hi from server') }, client: () => { console.log('hi from client') }, socket: () => { console.log('hi from server socket') } }
})) --- <dom-global-rules rules={staticallyParsedRules}></dom-global-rules>
<div onclick="thing.server()">log something on the server</div>
<div onclick="thing.socket()">log something on the socket</div>
<div onclick="thing.client()">log something on the client</div>
item.author: reggi

item.title: AWS EventBridge 概述 | AWS EventBridge Overview
item.description:
什么是 Amazon EventBridge？

该服务允许我们通过将多种服务（AWS 服务、自定义服务和第三方服务）连接在一起来开发完全由事件驱动的应用程序。

这个怎么运作

发布者将 JSON 事件发送到事件总线，要接收事件，您需要创建规则。 如果发布的事件与规则匹配，则该事件最多路由到五个目标。

事件桥功能

事件是系统、数据或环境中的实时变化。 此更改可以在您的应用程序中，也可以在 AWS 服务或 SaaS 合作伙伴服务中。

事件源

事件源用于从 SaaS 合作伙伴、AWS 服务或您自己的应用程序中提取事件。

活动巴士

事件总线是接收事件的管道。 与事件总线关联的规则在事件到达时对其进行评估。 每条规则都会检查事件是否符合规则的条件。

事件总线类型：

  1. 默认事件总线：创建默认总线并从任何 AWS 服务接收事件
  2. 自定义事件总线：从您创建的自定义应用程序接收事件，必须手动创建
  3. 合作伙伴事件总线：从 SaaS 合作伙伴应用程序接收事件，必须手动创建。

规则

规则匹配传入事件并将它们发送到目标进行处理。 单个规则可以将事件发送到多个目标，然后并行运行。 规则基于事件模式或时间表。

目标

目标是 EventBridge 在事件与为规则定义的事件模式匹配时向其发送事件的资源或端点。 该规则处理事件数据并将相关信息发送给目标。 要将事件数据传递到目标，EventBridge 需要访问目标资源的权限。 您最多可以为每个规则定义五个目标。

一旦暂停，norbybaru 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，norbybaru 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，norbybaru 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 norbybaru 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 norbybaru：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 norbybaru 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

What is Amazon EventBridge?

Amazon EventBridge is a new service that changed how we develop Serverless applications.

This service allow us to develop a fully event-driven application by connecting multiple services together - AWS services, custom services and third party services.

EventBridge was formerly called Amazon CloudWatch Events. Amazon CloudWatch Events and EventBridge are the same underlying service and API, however, EventBridge provides more features.

How it works

EventBridge delivers a stream of real-time data from AWS services, SaaS applications and your own applications to different types of targets without writing code.

The publisher sends a JSON event to an event bus, and to receive events, you create a rule. If the published event matches with a rule, the event is routed to up to five targets.

EventBridge is directly integrated with over 130 event sources and over 35 targets.

EventBridge Features

An event is a real-time change in a system, data, or environment. This change can be either in your application or in an AWS service or a SaaS partner service.

Event sources

An event source is used to ingest events from a SaaS partner, AWS Services, or your own applications.

Event buses

An event bus is a pipeline that receives events. Rules associated with the event bus evaluate events as they arrive. Each rule checks whether an event matches the rule’s criteria.

Event Bus Types:

  1. Default event bus: Default bus created and receives events from any AWS service
  2. Custom event bus: Receives events from custom applications you created and must be created manually
  3. Partner event bus: Receives events from SaaS partner applications and must be created manually.

Rules

A rule matches incoming events and sends them to targets for processing. A single rule can send an event to multiple targets, which then run in parallel. Rules are based either on an event pattern or a schedule.

Targets

A target is a resource or endpoint that EventBridge sends an event to when the event matches the event pattern defined for a rule. The rule processes the event data and sends the pertinent information to the target. To deliver event data to a target, EventBridge needs permission to access the target resource. You can define up to five targets for each rule.

Once suspended, norbybaru will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, norbybaru will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by norbybaru will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If norbybaru is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag norbybaru:

norbybaru consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging norbybaru will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 大 O 表示法：时间和空间复杂性 | Big O Notation: Time and Space Complexity
item.description:
维达基亚拉

发表于效率是当今的关键词。 作为人类，我们无论做什么都努力提高效率，从使用谷歌地图四处走动到在家门口送餐。

在计算中，计算复杂度是运行算法所需的资源量。 在这种情况下，资源是所需的时间和内存。

时间复杂度是算法所花费的时间量与输入长度的函数。 空间复杂度是算法运行时所占用的辅助内存，作为输入长度的函数。 辅助存储器是计算机系统中成本最低、空间最大、方法最慢的存储。

时间和空间至关重要，因为它们决定了如何开发、设计不同的计算机程序并为最终用户增加价值。

理想的算法是一种花费更少的时间和消耗
最小的空间。

就像人类优化任务一样，算法需要以最佳方式执行。 没有什么是一成不变的，时间和空间的大部分权衡都与需求有关。 如果算法 A 的重要一点是在内存使用情况下尽可能快地运行，我们选择最高效的时间。 然而，如果对于算法 A，最重要的事情是在时间上节约空间，我们选择最节省空间的。 此外，如果我们需要节省时间和空间，我们选择一个使用平均时间和空间量的。

时间和空间复杂度的一个很好的例子如下： 示例 1 打印一个简单的语句

 import time # get the start time st = time . time () print ( "checking the time complexity example" ) # get the end time et = time . time () # get the execution time elapsed_time = et - st print ( 'Execution time:' , elapsed_time , 'seconds' ) # output # checking the time complexity example # Execution time: 4.57763671875e-05 seconds

示例 2 打印一个简单的语句 10 次

import time # get the start time st = time . time () for i in range ( 10 ): print ( "time complexity example" ) # get the end time et = time . time () # get the execution time elapsed_time = et - st print ( 'Execution time:' , elapsed_time , 'seconds' ) # output # time complexity example # time complexity example # time complexity example # time complexity example # time complexity example # time complexity example # time complexity example # time complexity example # time complexity example # time complexity example # Execution time: 6.031990051269531e-05 seconds

两者的执行时间不同，因为示例一运行一个简单的语句（执行时间为 4.57763671875e-05 秒），而示例二有一个 FOR 循环，所用时间取决于 N 的值，在我们的例子中是 10 次（执行时间为 6.031990051269531e-05 秒）。

上面的两个例子清楚地说明了使用语句的算法执行一次并且需要相同的时间。 具有像循环这样的条件的语句将根据循环应该运行的次数而增加。 对于具有循环或嵌套循环和单个语句的组合的算法，时间根据每个语句应运行的次数按比例增加。

大 O 符号

随着输入大小的增加，我们使用 Big O 来表示算法相对于其输入的运行时间。

复杂性的类型

  1. 恒定时间 - O(1)
    当算法不依赖于输入大小时，它具有恒定的时间。 这意味着无论输入大小是否增加，运行时间都将始终相同。

  2. 线性时间 - O(n)
    当运行时间随着输入长度的增加而增加时，算法具有线性时间复杂度。

  3. 对数时间 – O (log n)
    当输入的大小在每一步中减小时，算法具有对数时间复杂度，这意味着输入大小与正在执行的操作不同。 操作随着输入大小的增加而增加。 这在二叉树或二叉搜索树中很常见，在搜索值时将数组一分为二，以确保不会对数据的每个元素都进行操作。

  4. 二次时间 - O (n^2)
    当时间随输入长度非线性增加 (n^2) 时，算法具有二次时间。 为了举例说明嵌套循环，请使用此循环，其中一个循环具有 O(n)，然后找到另一个循环，它适用于 O(n)*O(n) = O(n^2)。

如果说定义了“m”个循环，则顺序将为 O(n ^ m)。 这称为多项式时间复杂度函数。

所有时间复杂度的增长顺序

时间和空间复杂度的差异

时间空间
计算所用时间估计所需的内存
每一条语句都计算时间所有输出都计算内存
输入数据是主要决定因素可变大小是主要决定因素
对解决方案优化至关重要对解决方案优化至关重要

备忘单

通用数据结构空间和时间复杂度备忘单

数组排序算法空间和时间复杂度备忘单

结论

通过这篇文章，我们探讨了什么是时间和空间复杂度，以及为什么我们使用大 O 表示法。 无论您是出于好奇而第一次面试还是第一次学习这个，我希望这些知识能一直伴随着你。

资源

备忘单
时间复杂度

一旦未发布，veldakiara 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 veldakiara 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 veldakiara 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Velda Kiara

Posted on Oct 18

Efficiency is the word of the day. As humans we strive to be efficient in whatever we do, from moving around using Google Maps to having food delivered at our doorstep.

In computing, the computational complexity is the amount of resources required to run the algorithm. Resources in this case are the time and memory needed.

Time complexity is the amount of time taken by an algorithm as a function of the length of the input. Space complexity is the auxiliary memory taken by an algorithm to run as a function of the length of the input. Auxiliary memory is the lowest-cost, highest-space, and slowest-approach storage in a computer system.

Time and space are crucial since they determine how different computer programs are developed, designed and add value to the end user.

An ideal algorithm is one that takes less time and consumes
minimal space.

Algorithms just like humans optimizing tasks need to be executed optimally. Nothing is set in stone, most of the tradeoffs of time and space are in relation to the requirements. If the important thing of algorithm A is to run as fast as possible despite memory usage, we choose the most time efficient. While, if for the algorithm A, the vital thing is conserving space despite time, we select the most space efficient. Also, if we need to save both time and space we select one which uses an average amount of both time and space.

A good example of time and space complexity is as follows: Example 1 to print one simple statement

import time # get the start time
st = time.time() print("checking the time complexity example") # get the end time
et = time.time() # get the execution time
elapsed_time = et - st
print('Execution time:', elapsed_time, 'seconds') # output
# checking the time complexity example
# Execution time: 4.57763671875e-05 seconds

Example 2 to print one simple statement 10 times

import time # get the start time
st = time.time() for i in range(10): print("time complexity example") # get the end time
et = time.time() # get the execution time
elapsed_time = et - st
print('Execution time:', elapsed_time, 'seconds') # output
# time complexity example
# time complexity example
# time complexity example
# time complexity example
# time complexity example
# time complexity example
# time complexity example
# time complexity example
# time complexity example
# time complexity example
# Execution time: 6.031990051269531e-05 seconds

The execution time differs for both since example one runs a simple statement(execution time of 4.57763671875e-05 seconds) while example two has a FOR Loop and the time taken is subject to the value of N, which in our case is 10 times(execution time of 6.031990051269531e-05 seconds).

The two examples above clearly illustrate that for algorithms that use statements execute once and require same amount of time. While statements that have conditions like Loops will increase depending on the number of times the Loop should run. For algorithms with combinations of Loops or nested Loops and single statements, time increases proportionately based on the number of times each and every statement should run.

Big O Notation

Big O Notation is used to describe the complexity of an algorithm using algebra terms. We use Big O to express running time of algorithms relative to its input, as the input size increases.

Types of Complexities

  1. Constant time – O(1)
    An algorithm has constant time when it does not depend on the input size. Which means whether the input size increases or not, the runtime will always be the same.

  2. Linear time – O(n)
    An algorithm has linear time complexity when the run time increases with increase in the length of the input.

  3. Logarithmic time – O (log n)
    An algorithm has logarithmic time complexity when the size of input decreases in each step, which means that the input size is not the same as the operations being done. Operations increase as the input size increases. This is commonly seen in binary trees or binary search trees, where to search values you split the array into two, to ensure operation is not done on every element of the data.

  4. Quadratic time – O (n^2)
    An algorithm has quadratic time when the time increases non-linearly (n^2) with the length of the input. To exemplify nested Loops use this where one Loop has O(n), then finds another Loop which goes for O(n)*O(n) = O(n^2).

If say there are 'm' Loops defined, the order will be O(n ^ m). This is called polynomial time complexity functions.

Order of growth for all time complexities

Differences in Time and Space Complexity

Time Space
Calculates the time taken Estimates the memory required
Time is counted for each and every statement Memory is counted for all outputs
Input data is the main determinant The variable size is the main determinant
Vital for solution optimization Essential for solution optimization

Cheatsheets

Common Data structure Space and Time Complexity Cheatsheet

Array Sorting Algorithms Space and Time Complexity Cheatsheet

Conclusion

Through this article we have explored what time and space complexity and why we use Big O notation. Whether you are interviewing or learning this for the first time out of curiosity I hope this knowledge sticks with you.

Resources

Complexities
Guide to Complexity
Cheatsheet
Time Complexity
Complexity differences
Big O Notation

📚 Adjust your experience level to see more relevant content

item.title: Rails 贯穿 | Rails Run-Through
item.description:
很快

验证

验证存在于模型中，检查是否满足一个或多个特定参数，如果正确则返回预期结果。 在验证失败的情况下进行错误处理。

以下是一些简单的验证，包括一些丑陋的正则表达式：

 class Post < ApplicationRecord validates :title , presence: true validates :content , presence: true , length: { minimum: 50 } validates :category , inclusion: { in: [ 'Ruby' , 'Rails' ] } validates :capitalized? def capitalized? if title . split . first . match ( /\A[AZ]/ ) errors . add ( :title , "first letter must be capitalized" ) end end end

这些验证按顺序执行以下操作：

  1. 检查您的帖子是否有标题
  2. 验证内容是否存在，以及它的长度至少为 50
  3. 检查帖子的类别，确保值不为空，并且包含 Ruby 或 Rails
  4. 自定义验证：在验证下方定义方法后，现在检查标题是否以大写字母开头

如果其中任何一个验证不满足， ActiveRecord将抛出错误，并且尝试的任务失败。

序列化器

确保安装了 SERIALIZER GEM
自定义为单个模型返回的 json。 此功能类似于includes语句，其中与您正在操作的对象相关联的数据与它一起返回。

内置序列化器：

在Students的这个例子中，我将介绍一些合理的序列化器用例：

有了这个，每当我们为Student的任何实例发送 json 时，这个序列化程序就会天生适用于它。 此选项与自定义选项之间的主要区别在于在获取路径中传递序列化程序； 内置不需要在模型之外的其他地方编写，但自定义序列化程序必须。 首先，为Students创建序列化程序：

 rails g serializer students

例子：

现在，假设我们不想要所有这些值，而只想要学生的姓名和年龄（如果这是一个 SHOW 视图，我们绝对不需要 id，除非我们通过 id 以外的值进行搜索） . 使用序列化器，我们可以将这两个值作为属性传递

class StudentSerializer < ActiveModel :: Serializer attributes :name , :age has_many :books belongs_to :teacher end

我还进一步包括了关系，这是序列化程序的另一个方面，它们与 ActiveRecord 配对自动查找与外键关联的值。 在这里，在针对特定学生的 SHOW 请求中，我们将获取所有具有该学生外键（由该学生拥有）以及该学生所属的一位老师的书籍，并在 JSON 响应中返回它们。 这样的请求将返回如下内容：

 { "name" : "Frederick" , "age" : 21 , "books" : [ { "title" : "East of Eden" , "author" : "John Steinbeck" }, { "title" : "The House Of Mirth" , "author" : "Edith Wharton" } ], "teacher" : { "name" : "Mr. Teacher" , "class" : "Math" }
}

但是，这里返回的Teacher和Books的json包含它们的整个对象，如果我们想像对待Student一样序列化它怎么办？ 我们可以！ 向任一类添加序列化程序，就像我们为学生所做的一样，将天生将其应用于Teacher或Books的任何实例，即使再次与Student一起拉动。 这是书籍的示例序列化程序：

 class BookSerializer < ActiveModel :: Serializer attributes :title belongs_to :student end

现在，如果我们对单个学生执行相同的请求，则会返回：

 { "name" : "Frederick" , "age" : 21 , "books" : [ { "title" : "East of Eden" }, { "title" : "The House Of Mirth" } ], "teacher" : { "name" : "Mr. Teacher" , "class" : "Math" }
}

自定义序列化器：

假设我们希望学生获得相同的回报，但我们希望老师只包括他们的姓名。 从技术上讲，我们可以这样做：

 class TeacherSerializer < ActiveModel :: Serializer attributes :name has_many :students end

但这不是我们想要的。 当我们现在在教师本身上调用show方法时，它只会返回：

 { "name" : "Mr. Teacher"
}

但是如果我们想返回整个教师对象呢？ 如果我们只想在获取学生时让老师返回他们的名字，而在我们只想获取老师时继续返回整个对象怎么办？ 我们可以使用自定义序列化程序，然后传递是 IN student，belongs_to 所在的位置。

首先我们生成序列化器：

 rails g serializer student_teacher

然后，我们可以用一些代码填充它：

 class StudentTeacherSerializer < ActiveModel :: Serializer attributes :name has_many :students end

现在，没有教师的序列化器（不再有 TeacherSerializer），当我们对学生调用 SHOW 请求时，我们会得到：

 { "name" : "Frederick" , "age" : 21 , "books" : [ { "title" : "East of Eden" }, { "title" : "The House Of Mirth" } ], "teacher" : { "name" : "Mr. Teacher" }
}

哇。 只有老师的名字！ 好的。

错误处理

很快

最小化代码

很快



原文:

soon

Validations

Validations live in the model, checks if one or many certain parameters are met, and returns the expected result if correct. Error handling is done in the case of a validation failing.

Here are some simple validations, including some ugly regex:

class Post < ApplicationRecord validates :title, presence: true validates :content, presence: true, length: { minimum: 50 } validates :category, inclusion: { in: ['Ruby', 'Rails'] } validates :capitalized? def capitalized? if title.split.first.match(/\A[A-Z]/) errors.add(:title, "first letter must be capitalized") end end
end

These validations do the following, in order:

  1. Checks to see if your post has a title
  2. Validates that the content exists, as well as it has at least a length of 50
  3. Checks the post's category, making sure the value isn't empty, and includes either Ruby or Rails
  4. CUSTOM VALIDATION: after defining the method below the validations, this now checks to see if the title begins with a capitol letter

If any one of these validations is not met, an error will be thrown by ActiveRecord, and the attempted task has failed.

Serializers

MAKE SURE SERIALIZER GEM IS INSTALLED
Customizes the json returned for a single model. This functions like an includes statement, where data associated with the object you're acting on gets returned along with it.

Built-in Serializers:

In this example for Students, I will walk through some reasonable use-cases for serializers:

With this, whenever we send json for any instance of Student, this serializer will innately apply to it. The key difference between this and custom options is passing a serializer in a fetch path; Built-in doesn't need to be written elsewhere besides the model, but custom serializers must. (example later for custom)

First, create the serializer for Students:

rails g serializer students

Examples:

1) A student object contains these eight values:

create_table "students", force: :cascade do |t| t.string "name" t.integer "age" t.string "race" t.integer "grade" t.string "best-friend" t.integer "teacher_id"
end

Now, say we don't want all of those values, and instead only want the student's name and age (we definitely don't need an id if this is a SHOW view, unless we're searching by a value other than id). With a serializer, we can pass those two values as attributes

class StudentSerializer < ActiveModel::Serializer attributes :name, :age has_many :books belongs_to :teacher
end

I also went a step further to include the relationships, which is another aspect of serializers, where they automatically, in pair with ActiveRecord, find associated values with foreign keys. Here, in a SHOW request for a specific student, we are grabbing all books that have the student's foreign key (owned by the student), as well as the one teacher the student belongs to, and returning them in the JSON response. A request as such would return something like this:

{ "name": "Frederick", "age": 21, "books": [ { "title": "East of Eden", "author": "John Steinbeck" }, { "title": "The House Of Mirth", "author": "Edith Wharton" } ], "teacher": { "name": "Mr. Teacher", "class": "Math" }
}

Here, though, the returned json for Teacher and Books holds their entire object, what if we want to serialize it like we did with Student? We can! Adding a serializer to either class, the same as we did for student, will innately apply it to any instance of Teacher or Books, even when pulled with Student again. Here is an example serializer for books:

class BookSerializer < ActiveModel::Serializer attributes :title belongs_to :student
end

Now, if we do that same request for a single student, this will be returned:

{ "name": "Frederick", "age": 21, "books": [ { "title": "East of Eden" }, { "title": "The House Of Mirth" } ], "teacher": { "name": "Mr. Teacher", "class": "Math" }
}

Custom Serializers:

Say we want the same return from a student, but we want the teacher to only include their name. We could technically do it this way:

class TeacherSerializer < ActiveModel::Serializer attributes :name has_many :students
end

But this isn't what we want. When we call an show method on teacher itself now, it will only return this:

{ "name": "Mr. Teacher"
}

But what if we wanted to return the whole teacher object? What if we want to only have the teacher return their name when we fetch a student, but continue returning the whole object when we just want to fetch a teacher? We can use a custom serializer, then pass is IN student, where the belongs_to lives.

First we generate the serializer:

rails g serializer student_teacher

Then, we can populate it with some code:

class StudentTeacherSerializer < ActiveModel::Serializer attributes :name has_many :students
end

Now, without a serializer for teacher (no more TeacherSerializer), when we call a SHOW request on student we get this:

{ "name": "Frederick", "age": 21, "books": [ { "title": "East of Eden" }, { "title": "The House Of Mirth" } ], "teacher": { "name": "Mr. Teacher" }
}

WOW. Only the teacher's name! Nice.

Error handling

soon

Minimizing Code

soon
item.author: konstantinstanmeyer

去吃晚饭，然后试着记录下这次谈话。 希望我能快点把它击倒。 在那之后，我想在我的业余项目上完成一些工作，如果《电锯人》上映了，也许会看新一集。

item.title: 如何设置 SSH 密钥并链接到 GitHub | How to set up your SSH key and link to GitHub
item.description:
嘿初学者编码器！ 将 SSH 密钥链接到 GitHub 或 GitLab 对于更新存储库至关重要。 让我们跳进去。

在 Mac OS 上生成 SSH 密钥

如果您已经有 SSH 密钥，请跳到下一部分（在终端中输入ls -al ~/.ssh以查看是否有任何 ssh 密钥。您的 ssh 密钥将以.pub结尾）。

选择“输入”。

终端将提示您输入密码。 选择您选择的密码并输入。 确保在安全位置记下此密码。

在 Mac OS 上将 SSH 密钥添加到 SSH 代理

如果您之前已经这样做过，请跳至“将您的 SSH 密钥链接到 GitHub”。

在您的终端中键入以下内容：

  • 将 SSH 密钥链接到 GitHub
    1. 登录您的 GitHub/GitLab 帐户
    2. 导航到“设置”而不是“SSH 密钥”
    3. 选择“新建 SSH 密钥”
    4. 为您的密钥创建标题
    5. 粘贴您的公钥
    6. 单击“添加 SSH 密钥” ___

    你的公钥是什么？

    键入cat ~/.ssh/id_ed00000.pub或您各自密钥的名称（使用ls -al ~/.ssh查找文件名）

    终端将返回类似于ssh-ed... long_string_of_letters_and_numbers your_email的内容。

    仅将ssh-...和long_string_of_letters_and_numbers复制到 GitHub 上的公钥框中。

    而已！ 您已准备好将更改拉入并推送到您的存储库。

    有经验的编码员的问题！

    你有多少个 SSH 密钥？ 您是否为每个存储库创建一个新的 SSH 密钥？ 您是否对所有与工作相关的项目使用相同的 SSH 密钥？ 您建议生成多少个 SSH 密钥？ 在评论中告诉我。

    我错过了什么提示？ 让我知道！

    此致，初学者开发人员。

    资源：加州大学伯克利分校编码训练营和GitHub 。

    一旦暂停，malxx 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，malxx 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，malxx 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 malxx 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记malxx 的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

取消标记 malxx 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Hey beginner coders! Linking your SSH key to GitHub or GitLab is ESSENTIAL to updating your repositories. Let's jump into it.

Generating your SSH Key on Mac OS

If you already have an SSH key, skip to the next section (type ls -al ~/.ssh in your terminal to see if you have any ssh keys. Your ssh key will end in .pub).

ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your_email" The terminal will prompt you to enter a file "in which to save the key". Select "Enter".

The terminal will prompt you to enter a passcode. Choose a passphrase of your choice and enter. Make sure to note this passphrase in a secure location.

Add SSH key to SSH agent on Mac OS

If you've already done this previously, skip to "Linking Your SSH Key to GitHub".

In your terminal type the following:

  • sudo -s -H OR exec ssh-agent bash OR exec ssh-agent zsh (this depends on your environment)
  • eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
  • $ ssh-add --apple-use-keychain ~/.ssh/id_ed0000

Linking Your SSH Key to GitHub

  1. Log into your GitHub/GitLab account
  2. Navigate to "Settings" than "SSH keys"
  3. Select "New SSH key"
  4. Create a title for your key
  5. Paste your public key
  6. Click "Add SSH key" ___

What is your public key?

Type cat ~/.ssh/id_ed00000.pub OR the name of your respective key (find file name using ls -al ~/.ssh)

The terminal will return something that looks like ssh-ed... long_string_of_letters_and_numbers your_email.

Copy the ssh-... and the long_string_of_letters_and_numbers ONLY into the public key box on GitHub.

That's it! You're ready to pull and push changes to your repo.

QUESTION FOR EXPERIENCED CODERS!

How many SSH keys do you have? Do you make a new SSH key for every repository? Do you use the same SSH key for all work related projects? How many SSH keys do you recommend generating? Let me know in the comments.

Any tips that I missed? Let me know!

Sincerely, a beginner developer.

Resources: UC Berkeley Coding Boot Camp and GitHub.
